headline: Georgia's Ground Game Suffocates Washington in Convincing Week 8 Victory
body: Georgia's defense choked off Washington's rhythm from the opening kickoff, and when the Huskies tried to compensate by leaning on the pass, the Georgia secondary tightened the noose. Frank Fry's dominant performance at halfback—though the stat sheet credits Campbell with the touchdown—set the tone for a lopsided affair that never felt competitive. Georgia controlled the line of scrimmage on both sides, dictating tempo and field position throughout the afternoon.

Washington's passing attack, led by Williams Jr.'s efficient 22-of-33 completion rate and Davis's respectable six receptions, generated 240 yards but never translated that yardage into points. The Huskies were starved for rhythm in the red zone, and their three-carry, 24-yard rushing effort told the story: Georgia's front four made it impossible to establish anything inside. Young and Campbell finished off what was a methodical, suffocating performance with touchdown receptions that reflected the game's flow—Georgia was simply in control.

The two turnovers Georgia committed—both fumbles—might have seemed like a leak in an otherwise airtight performance, but they proved immaterial. Washington had no answer for the grinding, four-yard-at-a-time attack that consumed nearly 14 minutes per drive. By halftime, the psychological edge had already shifted decisively in Georgia's favor, and the second half became a formality.
turningPoint: Georgia's opening drive consumed nearly seven minutes and ended in a touchdown, establishing control of the line of scrimmage and forcing Washington into reactive football for the remainder of the contest.

unsungHero.name: Schaefer
unsungHero.why: While Fry earned Player of the Game, Schaefer's six tackles and sack quietly anchored a defensive performance that allowed only 279 yards and zero touchdowns, setting the foundation for the blowout.
